mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment decline, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and residential housing starts indicates broad-based demand expansion, which improves corporate earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index above 95.0 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization recovery, which lowers input cost pressures and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when INDPRO sustains values above 95.0 for three consecutive months, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging flows out of equities into commodities, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive months where CPIAUCSL increases exceed 0.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 48% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and declining real personal savings indicates consumption is being financed by debt rather than income growth, which raises default risk and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when PAYEMS rises and RSAFS falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP exceeding 2.0 percent annualized rate confirms economic expansion, which improves corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% for two consecutive quarters, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals a normalized labor market, which reduces fiscal stimulus expectations and triggers profit-taking in defensive sectors, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive when UNRATE falls below 9.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy exceeds 55% in the opposite direction, indicating a mis-specified regime mapping.
